turns.user_message: I was just sitting on the couch trying to unwind when my chest suddenly tightened and my breathing got shallow. It caught me completely off guard, even though I knew there was nothing actually wrong in the room. I have been noticing these little spikes lately, usually when I finally try to let my guard down. It is frustrating because I just want to relax, but my body keeps acting like it is waiting for something bad to happen. I end up pacing around the living room until the feeling slowly fades away.

That same tight feeling keeps showing up now, and it is starting to feel like a regular part of my routine. I will be doing something completely ordinary, like reading or making a snack, and suddenly my pulse races for no clear reason. It is exhausting trying to ignore it while I wait for the wave to pass on its own. I catch myself holding my breath and checking the time, wondering how long this round will last. The anticipation alone makes it harder to settle back into whatever I was doing before it hit.

Yesterday it hit me so hard that I actually had to sit on the floor just to catch my breath. My whole body felt completely wired and shaky, like I had just sprinted a mile, even though I had not moved from the couch. I tried to focus on the wall in front of me, but my thoughts kept spinning into this frantic loop. Every little sound in the house felt amplified, and I could not shake the heavy sense that something was terribly wrong. It took forever for my heart to finally slow down, and I am just drained from carrying this constant weight.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by referencing a specific recent moment. This has been going on for a while.
